events.title: 'US war on Iran not about the Iranian people: Europe can put the issue of human rights on the table'
events.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Mahmood Amiry-Moghaddam, President of Iran Human Rights and Professor of Neuroscience at University of Oslo. He offers a stark assessment of the Iranian regime's response to recent unrest and wartime conditions. Speaking from Oslo after the partial lifting of a prolonged internet blackout, Professor Amiry-Moghaddam argues that the authorities have exploited international attention on regional conflict to intensify domestic repression. Civilians are effectively "risking their lives" so that "the world can know what is happening in Iran," underscoring both the scale of repression and the resilience of civil society under authoritarian pressure.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'US war on Iran not about the Iranian people: Europe can put the issue of human rights on the table'

events.title: Mozambique says five citizens killed in ‘xenophobic attacks’ in South Africa
events.description: South African police confirm two deaths of Mozambicans in Mossel Bay as anti-immigration violence sweeps country Mozambique said five of its nationals were killed in “xenophobic attacks” in South Africa at the weekend and efforts were under way on Tuesday to repatriate hundreds of others. However, the South African police confirmed only that two Mozambicans had died in violence in the southern coastal town of Mossel Bay, the first killings to be officially linked to a wave of anti-migrant protests sweeping the country. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Mozambique says five citizens killed in ‘xenophobic attacks’ in South Africa

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Emergency meeting on Lebanon as Israeli attacks casts pall over Iran-US negotiations
events.description: The Security Council meets late on Monday at France’s request, as concern grows over escalating violence in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah amid warnings of Israeli strikes on Beirut’s southern suburbs, and confusion over the status of US-Iran peace talks linked to the faltering ceasefires. Follow live here from New York.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Emergency meeting on Lebanon as Israeli attacks casts pall over Iran-US negotiations

events.title: Mexico City police teargas teachers’ protest 10 days before World Cup
events.description: Teachers associated with CNTE union were marching toward Zócalo for salary raises and reversal of pension laws Riot police fired teargas at teachers who were marching toward Mexico City’s historic Zócalo plaza, just days before the square is expected to host the 2026 World Cup “Fan Fest”. The incident is the second time police have clashed with teachers in the past week, and more conflict is likely as Mexico City prepares to hold the opening game of the Fifa World Cup on 11 June. “This event will have to be suspended,” Filiberto Frausto, a union leader, told AFP, which witnessed police firing teargas on 1 June. “A cause like ours should be far above – it’s far more important than a little bit of distraction and fun.” Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mexico City police teargas teachers’ protest 10 days before World Cup

events.title: Israel's military eliminates Hamas terrorist who helped abduct American-Israeli hostage, 3 others
events.description: The IDF announced it killed a Hamas Nukhba terrorist who participated in the abduction of hostage Hersh Goldberg-Polin during the Oct. 7 attack.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Israel's military eliminates Hamas terrorist who helped abduct American-Israeli hostage, 3 others

events.title: Wildfires devastating richer areas but fewer hectares burned globally – study
events.description: ‘Megafires’ in California, Canada, South Korea and Europe in 2025, but changes to farming slowed spread in parts of Africa “Devastating” wildfires ripped across the wealthier parts of the world in 2025, a study has found, even as globally, the area ravaged by flames fell. Catastrophic blazes claimed lives, homes and jobs last year in California, Canada, Europe and South Korea. But the 335m hectares burned was the second-lowest since 2002, the review found, largely owing to the expansion of African farms that have fragmented landscapes and hampered the spread of large savannah fires. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Wildfires devastating richer areas but fewer hectares burned globally – study

events.title: Drone strikes apartment building in NATO member Romania as Russia attacks neighboring Ukraine
events.description: A drone entered Romanian airspace and struck an apartment building, causing an explosion and fire that injured multiple people, authorities said.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Drone strikes apartment building in NATO member Romania as Russia attacks neighboring Ukraine

events.title: Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave in hazardous rescue mission
events.description: Two still missing as divers make their way deeper into cave through muddy water and sharp rocks to find them Four more miners who were trapped in a flooded cave in Laos for 10 days have been freed by divers, but two people are still missing as rescuers continue to crawl through narrow, deluged tunnels and sharp rocks to find them. The first of the party of seven men was rescued on Friday in a perilous rescue mission which has required teams to drain water from the cave and navigate collapse hazards. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave in hazardous rescue mission

events.title: UN mission reports rising tensions along the Blue Line in Lebanon
events.description: Monday’s hastily convened meeting of the UN Security Council at the request of France in response to escalating violence in Lebanon between Israeli forces and Hezbollah militants has underlined deepening international concern as the conflict intensifies, despite ongoing US mediation efforts.

events.title: Hamas struggles to fill leadership ranks as Israel hunts Oct 7 terrorists
events.description: Hours after an Israeli airstrike killed Hamas military wing chief Mohammed Odeh, Gaza's Eid markets were full, revealing a disconnect with Hamas.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hamas struggles to fill leadership ranks as Israel hunts Oct 7 terrorists

events.title: UN urges world not to forget Rohingya refugees as aid cuts threaten vital support
events.description: As the world approaches nine years since the mass displacement of Rohingya refugees from Myanmar into Bangladesh, the UN refugee agency (UNHCR) has appealed to the international community not to abandon the 1.2 million refugees living in the country, most of them in camps in Cox’s Bazar.

events.title: Global conflicts push Sri Lanka's economy to the brink
events.description: Sri Lanka was careening from crisis to crisis, then came the Mideast war. People say they've got no buffer left.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Global conflicts push Sri Lanka's economy to the brink

events.title: Lebanon hospital strikes impact most vulnerable patients, warns WHO
events.description: The UN health agency in Lebanon is verifying reports of strikes on a hospital in the southern city of Tyre on Monday, amid a concerning rise in attacks on healthcare in the country.

events.title: DR Congo Ebola outbreak: Nurses discharged after full recovery
events.description: Four nurses who fell ill with Ebola in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) have been discharged from hospital after recovering from the often-fatal illness that sparked an international health alert.

events.title: Iran and the US trade strikes in the Persian Gulf, further testing the ceasefire
events.description: Kuwait briefly shut the country's main airport after Iranian drones heavily damaged it and killed one person, the latest in a series of attacks by Iran and the U.S. that have tested a fragile truce.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Iran and the US trade strikes in the Persian Gulf, further testing the ceasefire

events.title: Trump denies impasse in Iran talks after fresh strikes on Gulf countries
events.description: The ceasefire with Iran is being further tested after Iranian drones hit a terminal building in Kuwait. It forced the country to close down the airport briefly and one person was killed and at least 63 injured, according to the Kuwaiti health ministry. Meanwhile, talks with the US have been stalling over Lebanon and the US military said it had launched strikes on an island in the Strait of Hormuz.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Trump denies impasse in Iran talks after fresh strikes on Gulf countries

events.title: From Libyan deserts to 3D-printed guns: The weapons that never go away
events.description: Years after conflicts fade from the headlines, the weapons used to fight them often continue to circulate – crossing borders, fuelling crime and undermining an often-fragile peace. Now, ghost guns, 3D-printed firearms and increasingly sophisticated trafficking networks are creating new challenges for governments worldwide.

events.title: Georgia groom killed, bride injured on wedding night when helicopter crashes in woods
events.description: A Georgia wedding night helicopter sendoff reportedly turned fatal, killing the groom and pilot while the bride survived six hours in the wreckage.

events.title: Israel accuses UN of placing it on same sexual violence blacklist as Hamas terrorists, severs ties
events.description: Israeli officials blast the U.N. after accusing the body of placing Israel on a sexual violence blacklist that also includes Hamas and other terrorist groups.

events.title: One killed and dozens injured in Iranian drone strikes on Kuwait airport
events.description: Iran says the attack on Kuwait was in retaliation for earlier US strikes on an Iranian oil tanker and island.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ One killed and dozens injured in Iranian drone strikes on Kuwait airport

events.title: Iranian Revolutionary Guards claim attacks on Bahrain and Kuwait
events.description: The already fragile ceasefire between Iran and the US has come under strain following a series of attacks from Iran in the Gulf and from the US on Qeshm Island. The Iranian attacks killed one person and injured 63 others. FRANCE 24's Hoda Abdel-Hamid tells us more on the attacks.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iranian Revolutionary Guards claim attacks on Bahrain and Kuwait

events.title: 'Palestinians are unbelievable people: Vast majority of in Gaza just want to live a normal life'
events.description: Angela Diffly is pleased to welcome Dr. Adi Nadimpalli, Medical Coordinator for Gaza with Doctors Without Borders. He offers a stark assessment of conditions in Gaza following the October 2025 ceasefire. While acknowledging that "the number of kinetic injuries and violence has decreased," he argues that the territory remains trapped in a state of profound humanitarian deterioration, marked by daily strikes, mass displacement, inadequate food supplies, collapsing living conditions, and widespread insecurity.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Palestinians are unbelievable people: Vast majority of in Gaza just want to live a normal life'

events.title: UK government condemns violence at protest over teen's stabbing death
events.description: Britain’s interior minister accuses activists of hijacking a tragedy to stir up violence after police were attacked at a protest over a teenager's death

events.title: ‘Women and girls cannot wait’: Lebanon’s crisis is putting lives at risk
events.description: Women and girls in Lebanon are paying an increasingly devastating price as violence and displacement continue despite a ceasefire, the UN reproductive health agency (UNFPA) warned on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Women and girls cannot wait’: Lebanon’s crisis is putting lives at risk

events.title: New Aukus drone tech to protect critical undersea cables as Marles warns: ‘seabed is a battlefield’
events.description: Minister at Singapore defence summit also reveals Australia to buy only secondhand Aukus submarines from US Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The defence minister, Richard Marles, has said the “seabed is a battlefield” in a combative speech urging Beijing to be more transparent about its maritime operations, and taking aim at weak international controls over so-called “shadow-fleet” vessels. The warning came as the US, UK and Australia announced a new Aukus project to develop new underwater drone technology to protect undersea cables. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 New Aukus drone tech to protect critical undersea cables as Marles warns: ‘seabed is a battlefield’

events.title: Typhoon Jangmi threatens Japan as Europe swelters
events.description: Powerful winds and rain expected in parts of Japan and Australia, while temperatures in Spain could hit 40C A powerful tropical storm is forecast to track near Okinawa, Japan, on Monday before moving towards the south-east of the country. Typhoon Jangmi (also known as Typhoon No 6) has formed within the monsoonal gyre over the Philippine Sea. A monsoonal gyre is a large, slow-rotating weather system that spawns typhoons through smaller vortices formed within it. This flow can intensify storms. Such typhoons are typically characterised by broad areas of low pressure and extensive wind fields, often without a distinct eye. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Typhoon Jangmi threatens Japan as Europe swelters

events.title: India news: Several killed in New Delhi fire
events.description: At least 21 people were killed and several others injured after a fire tore through a building in New Delhi. Some of the victims were foreign nationals who had traveled to India for medical treatment. DW has more.

events.title: Israel and Hezbollah exchange fire amid new talks in Washington
events.description: Lebanon said Israeli strikes on June 3 killed at least nine people in the country's south, while the Israeli army says it has intercepted a "hostile aircraft" in its northern regions. This comes as a new round of talks is to be held in Washington. FRANCE 24's Renée Davis in Beirut and Noga Tarnopolsky in Jerusalem tell us more.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Israel and Hezbollah exchange fire amid new talks in Washington

events.title: Christian farming communities under siege as US report names Fulani militants Nigeria's deadliest threat

events.title: Ukraine drones hit Saint Petersburg as economic forum starts

events.title: Ukraine's attack on Saint Petersburg 'brings war back to Russia'
events.description: Volodymyr Zelensky called Kyiv's attack on Saint Petersburg a 'fair' response to the deadly Russian strikes on several Ukrainian cities a day earlier. It is also a testimony to Ukraine's growing capacity to strike further inside Russia. FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert tells us what that means for Russia.

events.title: Haiti: UN rights office welcomes new judicial units to tackle gang violence and impunity

events.title: UN deplores another wave of Russian attacks across Ukraine

events.title: What to know about U.S. military strikes on alleged drug boats
events.description: The U.S. military strikes on alleged drug boats have killed over 200 people. The attacks began in September and aim to stem drug flow, but critics question their legality and effectiveness.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 What to know about U.S. military strikes on alleged drug boats

events.title: UK: Protest erupts over student murder
events.description: Protesters have clashed with police in Southampton over the December murder of finance student Henry Nowak. The fatally injured victim was handcuffed by police after his killer accused him of racist abuse.

events.title: UN General Assembly elects Bangladesh’s Rahman as next president
events.description: Bangladeshi Foreign Minister Khalilur Rahman was elected President of the UN General Assembly’s 81st session on Tuesday after defeating Andreas Kakouris of Cyprus in a closely contested vote, positioning himself to steer the world body through a pivotal year marked by intensifying global crises, UN reform efforts and major leadership transitions.

events.title: Cricket Canada suspended over allegations of gang-linked corruption
events.description: ICC’s decision comes amid growing concerns the team is being influenced by members of a notorious gang in India Cricket’s international governing body has suspended Canada over what it described as “serious breaches of its membership obligations”, dealing the latest blow to an organization that critics say has become a “laughing stock” within the sport. The suspension also comes amid growing concerns that one of Canada’s fastest-growing sports is being influenced by members of a notorious gang that operates with impunity from an Indian prison cell . Continue reading...

events.title: Japan defence minister rebuffs claims of ‘new militarism’ levelled by China
events.description: Shinjiro Koizumi says Japan valued as a ‘peace-loving’ nation while China expands military capabilities ‘without sufficient transparency’ Japan’s defence minister took a veiled swipe at China on Sunday, pledging to keep strengthening the military despite Beijing’s criticism of Tokyo’s increasingly muscular security stance. Under the prime minister, Sanae Takaichi , Japan has quickened its pivot to a more proactive defence policy, further shaking off – with US encouragement – its pacifist outlook in place since the end of the second world war. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Japan defence minister rebuffs claims of ‘new militarism’ levelled by China

events.title: Algeria court clears way for pardon of jailed French journalist Christophe Gleizes
events.description: French sports journalist Christophe Gleizes, detained in Algeria since 2024 on terror charges, may be eligible for a possible presidential pardon, his lawyers said Wednesday. Algeria's Court de Cassation, the country's highest appeals court, rejected prosecutors' calls for a tougher sentence, and noted the withdrawal of the defendant's own appeal.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Algeria court clears way for pardon of jailed French journalist Christophe Gleizes

events.description: At least 26 Palestinians were killed in the Gaza Strip since Tuesday – the eve of one of the most important holidays in Islam – the UN human rights office, OHCHR, reported on Friday.

events.title: UK will not have to pay Rwanda £100m over failed asylum scheme, court rules
events.description: Rwanda had sued UK government over alleged breach of agreement, after scheme scrapped by Labour on first day in office The UK will not have to pay the Rwandan government millions of pounds over a failed migrant deportation scheme set up by Boris Johnson’s administration, an international court has ruled. The east African nation had sued the current UK government for more than £100m, claiming it was owed after a breach of an agreement. Continue reading...

events.title: Musket balls and a burnt hull: evidence of real pirates of the Caribbean found in Bahamas
events.description: Exclusive: First shipwrecks found in Nassau harbour on New Providence, once the hideout of Blackbeard and Calico Jack The first shipwrecks linked to the real pirates of the Caribbean in the Bahamas have been discovered by an international team co-directed by a British marine archaeologist. Blackbeard and Calico Jack Rackham were among pirates who, between the 1690s and 1720s, turned Nassau on the island of New Providence into a hideout where they plotted their next heists on the high seas and divided up their plunder. Continue reading...

The legislation, which was passed on Friday, mandates prison sentences of three to 10 years. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 People ‘panicking’ as Ghana passes sweeping law criminalising LGBTQ+ activity

events.title: ‘We don’t have another country to run to’: Kenyans fear US plan for Ebola quarantine site
events.description: People from town of potential site for US citizens exposed to Ebola say it puts them at risk in country with no known cases People from a town in central Kenya where the US wants to set up an Ebola quarantine facility for its citizens have strongly criticised the plan, saying they fear it will expose them to the virus and that it is indicative of double standards on the part of the US. “Everybody should be quarantined in their home country. We shouldn’t allow foreigners to bring us diseases,” said Charles Mathenge, a taxi driver who lives near Laikipia Air Base, the proposed site in Nanyuki, 120 miles from the capital, Nairobi. “Kenya is our country, and we should be careful with it.” Continue reading...

events.title: EU strikes migration deal for more deportations and detention centers abroad

events.title: New York police investigate videos of men emerging from city's sewer system
events.description: Authorities warn that entering the sewer system is both illegal and extremely dangerous.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 New York police investigate videos of men emerging from city's sewer system

events.title: British couple lose Iran jail sentence appeal, family says
events.description: Lindsay and Craig Foreman were arrested in January 2025 during a global motorbike journey.

events.title: Which World Cup 2026 stadiums will host matches in US, Canada and Mexico?
events.description: A quick look at the 16 venues across 16 cities in three countries, which will host the biggest-ever FIFA World Cup.

events.title: Lamine Yamal and Nico Williams set to be fit for Spain’s World Cup opener
events.description: Barcelona's Lamine Yamal and Athletic's Nico Williams are expected to shake injuries in time to face Cape Verde.

events.title: The Population Bust
events.description: A revealing global journey into declining birth rates, ageing societies, and their far-reaching impact.

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: Trump administration proposes 25% tariffs on Brazil despite US trade surplus
events.description: US claims world’s 10th-biggest economy engages in ‘unreasonable’ trade practices that ‘restrict US commerce’ Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Trump administration proposed 25% tariffs on imports from Brazil , charging that the world’s 10th-biggest economy engages in trade practices that are “unreasonable’’ and that “burden or restrict US commerce”. Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva said he received the decision “with indignation”. The Brazil president also blamed the decision by the US administration on his rival in October’s elections, Flávio Bolsonaro , the senator who visited Washington last week. The senator is the son of former president Jair Bolsonaro, once nicknamed “the Trump of the Tropics” by his allies. Continue reading...

events.title: Ultra-Orthodox protesters block roads and trains across Israel over military draft
events.description: Tens of thousands of ultra-Orthodox demonstrated across Israel on Monday, blocking roads and trains and setting cars on fire to protest mandatory enlistment in Israel's military.

events.title: Saudi Arabia and Russia draw closer amid OPEC upheaval
events.description: As Saudi Arabia and Russia deepen cooperation, their combined influence over global oil supply is growing. Could this shift the balance of power in energy markets?

events.title: World Cup: David Beckham to get Hollywood Walk of Fame star
events.description: Former Manchester United and England superstar to be honored for role in "elevating soccer's profile in America" ahead of FIFA World Cup.

events.title: Shnaider beats Sabalenka at French Open to make semi-finals
events.description: World No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka was eliminated on Wednesday in the quarterfinals of the French Open, losing to Russia's Diana Shnaider (3-6, 7-5, 6-0).

events.title: Ukrainian drones hit Saint Petersburg as 'Russian Davos' opens in the city
events.description: Ukrainian long-range drones hit an oil terminal in the Russian city of St Petersburg on June 3, setting the facility ablaze. It came hours before Vladimir Putin's showcase economic forum got under way in the city, apparently an attempt to embarrass the Kremlin chief. Moscow said it would keep striking Ukraine systematically in response to such attacks.

events.title: Raúl Castro turns 95 as Cuba celebrates enigmatic figure
events.description: Raúl Castro has turned 95, a landmark birthday for a man still helping lead one of the last communist countries in the world

events.title: Under fire, Kharkiv is already building for a peaceful tomorrow
events.description: Every day in Kharkiv begins with uncertainty: air raid sirens interrupt sleep; missiles strike residential neighbourhoods, industrial sites, and roads. Anxious citizens rush into metro stations during bombardments and children study underground.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Under fire, Kharkiv is already building for a peaceful tomorrow

events.title: Eight killed after drone hits bus in Russian-occupied part of Ukraine
events.description: The bus was hit in the eastern Donetsk region, an official says.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Eight killed after drone hits bus in Russian-occupied part of Ukraine

events.title: Kuwait and Bahrain attacked as Iran launches missile and drone barrage
events.description: Kuwait and Bahrain attacked as Iran launches missile and drone barrage
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Kuwait and Bahrain attacked as Iran launches missile and drone barrage

events.title: Is Asia facing a new currency crisis?
events.description: A stronger dollar, higher energy costs and market uncertainty are putting pressure on currencies across Asia.

events.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
events.description: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

events.title: Diphtheria outbreak: residents of remote NT community say health clinic has no hand sanitiser
events.description: There’s a three-week wait on test results in Yuendumu, near Alice Springs, and locals say NT Health has dropped the ball on telling locals what to do if they test positive Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A remote Aboriginal community at the centre of the Northern Territory’s diphtheria outbreak is struggling to cope with rising case numbers, with locals saying there is no hand sanitiser at the health clinic and limited information about how to avoid the disease or what to do if you test positive. There have been more than 240 cases of the once-eradicated disease reported in Australia since October, primarily in remote Indigenous communities in the NT, South Australia, Queensland and Western Australia , according to data released by the Australian Centre for Disease Control. One of the largest clusters in the NT is in Yuendumu, a community of about 700 people 300km from Alice Springs. Continue reading...

events.title: Backrooms: The creepy internet phenomenon, explained
events.description: A low-budget horror film directed by a 20-year-old YouTuber is beating the new Star Wars movie at the box office. Behind its success is an entire viral online maze.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Backrooms: The creepy internet phenomenon, explained

events.title: Who owns the billions in Germany's dormant bank accounts?
events.description: Billions of euros sit untouched in dormant bank accounts in Germany. With heirs unaware and no central register in place, banks and politicians are debating who should ultimately control the money.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Who owns the billions in Germany's dormant bank accounts?

events.title: Democracy in Africa: Coups, crises and foregone conclusions
events.description: As Ethiopia formally counts the votes following Monday's election, attitudes toward democracy appear to be shifting across Africa. In April, Burkina Faso's junta leader told the Burkinabe people to "forget" democracy.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Democracy in Africa: Coups, crises and foregone conclusions

events.title: 'Keir Starmer made us promises - and we thought they'd be kept'
events.description: Two boys from Gaza who received medical treatment in the UK have said the prime minister has "broken a promise" to reunite them with their families and bring over hundreds of injured children.

events.title: UN calls on Nicaragua to investigate Indigenous leader Brooklyn Rivera’s death in custody
events.description: The United Nations has called on Nicaragua to investigate the death of Indigenous leader Brooklyn Rivera

events.title: Police in Italy arrest 2 people after apparent torching of a car kills 4 farmworkers
events.description: Italian police have arrested two people accused in the deaths of four migrant farmworkers who were burned alive in a car

events.title: Bakersfield, California bank standoff ends with hostage-taker dead after FBI-involved shooting: police
events.description: The hostage episode that began on Tuesday, ended on Wednesday with the suspect dead and hostages unharmed, according to the Bakersfield Police Department.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Bakersfield, California bank standoff ends with hostage-taker dead after FBI-involved shooting: police

events.title: Hezbollah's 'game changing' night-hunting weapon punches through Israel's defenses: expert
events.description: Drone expert warns Hezbollah's nighttime drone capabilities are game changing, using thermal sensors to target IDF troops under cover of darkness.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hezbollah's 'game changing' night-hunting weapon punches through Israel's defenses: expert

events.title: English cops cuffed teen stabbing victim after attacker claimed racial assault
events.description: Police apologize after handcuffing stabbing victim Henry Nowak based on attacker's alleged racism claim, sparking outrage over British policing culture.

events.title: Fireworks reportedly trigger mass horse stampede through Rome streets, injuring several soldiers
events.description: Unauthorized fireworks reportedly triggered a mass horse stampede during a late-night rehearsal for Italy's Republic Day parade in Rome, injuring riders.

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: Chinese dissident says he was berated by ‘pro-regime’ interpreter for UK police
events.description: Hong Qi, who orchestrated protest against Communist government, claims interpreter on 101 call launched political tirade A Chinese dissident who orchestrated an anti-government protest in China after fleeing to the UK has claimed that a “pro-regime” interpreter used by a British police force berated him when he sought help. Hong Qi, who made headlines last year after using a mobile phone while in the UK to remotely project anti-regime slogans on to a building in his home city, Chongqing, contacted police after discovering that his bank accounts had been frozen. Continue reading...

events.title: Nicotine use soars by 40% in eight years as Australian black market explodes
events.description: Australian Bureau of Statistics estimates nicotine consumption rose 40%, with illicit sources accounting for 80% of all tobacco Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australians are consuming more nicotine than they were eight years ago but are spending less, new data from the Australian Bureau of Statistics shows, as 80% of the cigarettes smoked by the nation last year were cheaper illegal products. The analysis released by the Australian Bureau of Statistics (ABS) on Wednesday showed the amount of nicotine consumed around the country soared by 40% between 2017 and 2025, despite population growth rising by just 14%. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 📈 Nicotine use soars by 40% in eight years as Australian black market explodes

events.title: Greetings from Porto, whose lanes are lined with colorful textiles
events.description: Linen scarves, cotton aprons and dishtowels adorn the entrances to souvenir shops, many of which are run by Bangladeshis whose home country shares Portugal's rich tradition of textile manufacturing.

events.title: Ukrainian drones strike a St. Petersburg oil terminal ahead of Putin visit
events.description: Ukrainian drones struck an oil terminal in St. Petersburg and set it ablaze, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said, as the city hosted an annual economic forum promoted by President Putin.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukrainian drones strike a St. Petersburg oil terminal ahead of Putin visit

events.title: Armenia's election offers a choice between Russia and EU
events.description: Armenians are set to vote on the country's future geopolitical direction on June 7. The election will be a test of public opinion in the long-standing Russia ally, where the population is increasingly eyeing the EU.

events.title: Film show: 'DJ Ahmet' charms with sheep, grief and a rave
events.description: Critic Emma Jones tells us why North Macedonian production "DJ Ahmet", starring first-time actors, is a gem of an independent movie and proof that the small country punches well above its weight when it comes to cinema. We also discuss unsettling thriller "The Plague", starring Joel Edgerton, as a group of teenage boys at a swimming camp prompts comparisons with "Lord of The Flies"-style dynamics. Plus, body horror "Saccharine" explores contemporary obsessions with weight loss and body image, and the "Scary Movie" horror spoof franchise returns for a sixth outing, 26 years after its Y2K debut.

events.title: US finds huge tunnel on the Mexican border used to smuggle drugs
events.description: Mexican authorities have discovered an illegal tunnel that is 600 metres long on the US border with Mexico. It links Tijuana with the city of San Diego. Its purpose? Possibly the illegal smuggling of drugs. The discovery was the result of a six-month long investigation.

events.title: New prime minister says Solomon Islands will review its secretive security treaty with China
events.description: The Solomon Islands' new leader says the country will review its secretive security treaty with China

events.title: Bomb threat standoff at Bakersfield Chase Bank continues as negotiators work to release hostages
events.description: A man allegedly made bomb threats at a Chase Bank in downtown Bakersfield, California, barricading himself inside with people as negotiators worked.

events.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon but partial truce with Hezbollah appears to hold
events.description: Israel continues its campaign in the south, though has not struck Beirut after a deal announced by the US.

events.title: Indonesia's Prabowo fires head of free meals scheme plagued by poisonings
events.description: The centrepiece programme of Prabowo's government has left tens of thousands of school children ill.

events.title: Does UK’s new far-right party, Restore, pose a threat to Farage’s Reform?
events.description: Restore Britain’s manifesto has pledged to implement the 'most ambitious programme of mass deportations'.

events.title: Video: Teachers in northern Nigeria protest abduction of school children
events.description: Teachers in north-eastern Nigeria held protests, demanding stronger protection for learning institutions.

events.title: Israel is building more military posts in Gaza, satellite imagery shows
events.description: An investigation by Al Jazeera has identified 40 distinct Israeli military outposts entrenched within Gaza.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Israel is building more military posts in Gaza, satellite imagery shows

events.title: Kidnappings, threats and ‘protection fees’: how can Mexico confront rise in deadly extortion?
events.description: With corrupt police on the streets and shopkeepers forced to pay gangs, president has vowed to tackle crime that now affects all parts of society It was about 11pm and Luis* was about to get into an Uber to go home when the police car pulled up. One of the officers frisked him and produced two plastic bags with what looked like drugs: one contained some sort of powder, the other little crystals. Luis had never seen them before. Luis, who asked not to use his real name for fear of reprisals, insisted that the drugs weren’t his, but the officers didn’t seem to care. They shoved him into the back of the police truck and drove into the night. Continue reading...

events.title: Trump threatens tariffs on 60 trading partners including UK and Canada over ‘forced labour’
events.description: Proposal for 10-12.5% levies, to also include EU, Taiwan and Australia, would allow US president to skirt court-imposed limits Donald Trump has threatened tariffs of between 10% and 12.5% on 60 trading partners including the UK, the EU and Australia over alleged forced labour failures, in the latest attempt to revive his signature trade policy. The EU immediately hit back, saying it expected the US to respect the tariff deal it entered into last July and arguing that stealth tariffs breached the spirit of that agreement. Continue reading...

events.title: Portugal: General strike causes disruption to services
events.description: Flights have been canceled and schools closed as unions stage a strike over government labor reform plans. It is the second major walkout in Portugal in six months.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Portugal: General strike causes disruption to services

events.title: Under Paris's Notre Dame, archaeologists are unearthing 2,000 years of history
events.description: Since Notre Dame Cathedral reopened to the public in 2024 following a devastating fire, it has been one of the most popular tourist destinations in Paris. What many don’t realize is that the work continues near the cathedral underground. To make way for trees and shade on the square in front of the church, workers have undertaken a significant archaeological dig.

events.title: Two dead in Kenya after protests against US planned Ebola quarantine zone
events.description: Two people have died in Kenya during protests against a US-proposed quarantine facility for Ebola patients, according to the organiser.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Two dead in Kenya after protests against US planned Ebola quarantine zone

events.title: UK Royal Navy says 3 members died in a helicopter crash during a training exercise

events.ticker_label: 💥 UK Royal Navy says 3 members died in a helicopter crash during a training exercise

events.title: Fire sweeps through a New Delhi building, killing at least 21 people
events.description: A fire has swept through a building in New Delhi, killing at least 21 people and injuring several others

events.title: Nancy Guthrie sheriff defends pace of investigation more than 4 months into search
events.description: The sheriff overseeing the Nancy Guthrie suspected abduction case blames lab work and scientific protocols for delays as it enters month four.

events.title: Fox News True Crime Newsletter: American woman's Bahamas disappearance treated as murder investigation
events.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News True Crime Newsletter, which brings you the latest cases ripped from the headlines, from crime to courts, legal and scandal.

events.title: US Coast Guard arrives in Bahamas to search for body in Lynette Hooker investigation
events.description: The U.S. Coast Guard has arrived in the Bahamas for Lynette Hooker's vanishing case. She disappeared while with her husband Brian Hooker.

events.title: Pentagon hosts first-ever Israeli–Lebanese military talks aimed at curbing Hezbollah
events.description: Israeli and Lebanese military delegations open U.S.-brokered talks at the Pentagon focused on ceasefire enforcement and border stability amid tensions.

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Germany loses vote for UN Security Council seat
events.description: Fifteen of the 193 UN member states sit on the UN Security Council. Germany was in competition with Austria and Portugal for two seats in the "Western Europe and Others" group.

events.title: A year before the French election, De Gaulle remains politically fashionable
events.description: ‘De Gaulle: Resistance’, is released in cinemas in France on June 3, the first part of a two-parter on Charles de Gaulle, the French general and statesman who led the Free French Forces against Nazi Germany and Vichy France. The much anticipated films took director Antonin Baudry and his team six years to make, with a budget of nearly 80 million euros. But even beyond the silver screen, with a year to go until the presidential elections, De Gaulle has perhaps never been so politically fashionable.

events.title: If Cuba collapses, Trump will be forced to deal with the consequences of his actions
events.description: It's only a couple of months or so since I was last in Cuba, and conditions then were pretty dire: power cuts, large queues for bread and fuel, state-run grocery stores virtually empty, mounting piles of rubbish on street corners, and few foreign visitors.

events.title: Canada calls for US and Mexico free trade agreement to be renewed for another 16 years
events.description: Canada is calling on the United States and Mexico to renew the free trade agreement between the countries for another 16 years just as U.S. President Donald Trump revives talk of making Canada the 51st state

events.title: Auburn University student disappears in Japan as parents join search: ‘In our own living hell’
events.description: American college student James "Weston" Higginbotham, 20, disappeared near Kyoto, Japan, as police focus the search on wooded hiking trails in the area.

events.title: Street takeover arrests surge across US as summer kicks off with social media-driven crime trend
events.description: Police across multiple states made arrests tied to street takeovers as the social media-driven crime trend intensifies heading into summer months.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Street takeover arrests surge across US as summer kicks off with social media-driven crime trend

events.title: Anti-cartel candidate 'The Tiger' channels Trump and Bukele in Colombia election shocker
events.description: Analysts say Colombia's presidential election signals growing regional rejection of leftist governments as voters prioritize security and counternarcotics.

events.title: US announces new tariffs over forced labour concerns
events.description: It comes after the US Supreme Court struck down many of US President Donald Trump's previous duties in February.

events.title: Microsoft says new quantum chip 1,000 times more reliable than predecessor
events.description: The tech giant predicts it will have a quantum computer that can solve commercially useful problems by the end of the decade.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: Mapping the AI narrative in Kenya and South Africa's media
events.description: From data colonialism to deepfakes, AI is reshaping Africa. A new study shows where Kenyan and South African coverage falls short, and offers practical steps to deepen and improve reporting.

events.title: Wembanyama chases NBA dream ahead of Spurs-Nicks final
events.description: The NBA finals start on June 4 and the San Antonio Spurs, with its star player, Victor Wembanyama, are favoured to take the title. But as the 22-year-old Frenchman told reporters, it's not a done deal yet against the Knicks - who haven't won since 1973 but won 11 consecutive games in the playoffs this year.

events.title: Moscow, Taliban forge military alliance in power grab after US Afghanistan exit: reports
events.description: Russia and Afghanistan's Taliban government reportedly signed a military cooperation pact, solidifying Moscow's growing alliance in Central Asia.

events.title: Ukrainian drones hit St Petersburg as Putin's flagship economic forum opens
events.description: Several long-range drones struck oil storage facilities near the city, days before Vladimir Putin is due to address the event.

events.title: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass advances to run-off in race to run California's biggest city
events.description: The mayor's opponent could be either Republican newcomer and reality TV star Spencer Pratt, or a fellow Democrat, council member Nithya Raman.

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.title: Russia: Ukraine drones hit Saint Petersburg
events.description: Ukrainian drones hit energy and military sites in Saint Petersburg early Wednesday morning as officials gathered for a flagship economic forum in the city, Russian and Ukrainian authorities said.

events.title: Exit polls suggest landslide win by South Korea's ruling liberal party in local elections
events.description: Exit polls show South Korea’s ruling liberal party has been projected to win a landslide victory in mayoral and other local elections

events.title: Shell pumped oil through Nigeria pipeline for years despite pollution evidence, documents show
events.description: The oil giant says the documents ignore the critical context of the complex operating environment at the time.

events.description: The $1.8bn fund would have paid people the Trump administration decided were unjustly and politically targeted.

events.title: Turkiye’s top diplomat, Indonesia’s president discuss $10bn trade goal
events.description: The two discuss a range of sectors, including defence, energy, transportation and the halal food industry.

events.title: Pro-Palestine US army veteran Adam Hamawy wins New Jersey primary
events.description: A former US Army combat surgeon who volunteered at Gaza hospital has a Democratic primary in New Jersey.

events.description: Charles Leclerc has extended his contract with Ferrari for an undisclosed length, having joined the F1 team in 2019.

events.title: Still haven't filed your taxes? Here's what you need to know

events.description: This is it.

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation

events.title: NSW motorists who use medicinal cannabis may soon be able to drive without fear of major penalty
events.description: Premier Chris Minns says changes would balance road safety and a more practical approach for medicinal cannabis users Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Motorists who use medicinal cannabis may soon be able to drive on New South Wales roads without fear of a severe penalty as the Minns government announces long-awaited reforms. The government announced on Thursday it would introduce legislation which would see drivers with a medicinal cannabis prescription no longer face a three-month licence suspension or fine for having the THC component of cannabis in their system. Continue reading...

events.title: Anti-abortion activists are trying to limit access in NSW – and they are just getting started
events.description: Obstetricians and gynaecologists say anti sex-selective abortions bill ‘predicated on misinformation’ and ‘underlying aim is to restrict access to abortion’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The man who wants to ban “sex-selective abortions” is the first person who will tell you it won’t work. New South Wales Libertarian party MLC John Ruddick has introduced legislation that would see health practitioners sent to prison or fined thousands if they carry out a termination because of the sex of a foetus. Continue reading...

events.title: German hotel apologizes for 'no Jews allowed' message

events.title: Taliban and Russia cozy up to each other — why?
events.description: Russia and the Afghan Taliban have signed a deal on repairing Soviet-era and Russian-made weapons, a seemingly small move in a larger game involving the US, Pakistan, and Moscow's efforts to curb Western influence.

events.title: Wim Wenders withdraws film with disputed child nude scene
events.description: Actress Nastassja Kinski spent years calling for the removal of a sexualized scene from his film "The Wrong Move," in which she appeared topless at the age of 13.

events.title: Google mosquito army: Scientists say 'we must take action'
events.description: Google's Debug research program plans to release millions of sterile mosquitoes to fight species that spread diseases like dengue. How does the method work — and should humans interfere with nature like this?

events.title: Germany news: Naturalizations reach record high in 2025
events.description: Naturalizations have increased for the fifth year in a row. Automotive industry firms turn a bit less pessimistic. And Germany is aiming for a seat on the UN Security Council. Follow DW for more news from Germany.

events.title: US plans extra tariffs for 60 countries over forced labor
events.description: A new wave of Trump tariffs could unsettle US trade partners which have already signed new trade deals or have been negotiating them for months.

events.title: Director Wim Wenders pulls 1975 film featuring then-teenager Nastassja Kinski in nude scene
events.description: German director Wim Wenders announced Wednesday that he would be pulling his 1975 film “Wrong Move” from circulation due to a scene in which actress Nastassja Kinski, then a teenager, appeared topless.

events.title: France arrests Russian captain of Moscow-linked oil tanker
events.description: The captain of the Tagor, a tanker suspected of belonging to Russia’s “ghost fleet” that was boarded in the Atlantic Ocean by the French Navy, was taken into custody on Tuesday, announced Brest prosecutor Stéphane Kellenberger.

events.title: AI chatbot responses polluted by pro-Russian disinformation
events.description: AI-driven chatbots are increasingly being used as sources of information, but they are also vulnerable to disinformation campaigns. Experts have found that pro-Russian misinformation, in particular, can seep into the responses generated by these conversational agents.

events.title: Paris's Saint-Germain-des-Prés book fair returns
events.description: The Saint-Germain-des-Prés book fair is a literary rendez-vous at the famous Parisien café, Les Deux Magots. To the delight of book lovers, the event is returning for its third year, bringing a rich and modern programme to the Left Bank historical venue where Boris Vian, Jean-Paul Sartre and Ernest Hemingway once gathered. It's a new chapter in the neighbourhood's literary tradition, inspired by a bygone era. In Entre Nous, Angela Diffley speaks to Gaëlle Le Gall Nicolas, marketing director of Les Deux Magots.

events.title: Shnaider beats Sabalenka at French Open to make semi-finals
events.description: No description available.

events.title: British couple jailed in Iran lose appeal, family says
events.description: A British couple jailed on spying charges in Iran have lost an appeal against their convictions, their family has said.

events.title: Australian court bans man from contacting Norwegian princess studying in Sydney
events.description: A court in Australia has banned a 63-year-old man from contacting Norway's Princess Ingrid Alexander or her family for two years as she studies at university in Sydney

events.title: A Hong Kong artist tries to mark the Tiananmen crackdown. He was quickly stopped by police
events.description: A performance artist in Hong Kong has tried to display a red thread to remember the victims of the 1989 Tiananmen Square crackdown
events.ticker_label: 💥 A Hong Kong artist tries to mark the Tiananmen crackdown. He was quickly stopped by police

events.title: Germany seizes tons of cocaine and suspects are arrested in Spain
events.description: German authorities have seized over 8 metric tons of cocaine from a container meant to carry cacao beans

events.title: Australian judges weigh Indigenous activist’s bid to prosecute King Charles for genocide
events.description: Three Australian appeals court judges have reserved their decision on whether an activist can prosecute King Charles III for alleged genocide of Australia’s Indigenous people

events.title: Former Thai prime minister Thaksin Shinawatra freed early from parole after receiving royal pardon
events.description: Thailand’s former Prime Minister Thaksin Shinawatra has completed his obligations related to a prison sentence

events.title: European Union observers reject Petro’s fraud claims, calling Colombia’s vote 'transparent'
events.description: The European Union’s Electoral Observation Mission to Colombia has discarded President Gustavo’s Petro’s claims of fraud in Sunday’s presidential election

events.title: Borrowed boots, borrowed joy: Football thrives in Gaza camps
events.description: Matches organised by a former professional player are providing a brief respite from the harsh reality of life for the thousands living in overcrowded tents, schools or damaged buildings in the shattered Occupied Palestinian Territory of Gaza.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Borrowed boots, borrowed joy: Football thrives in Gaza camps

events.title: Karen Bass to face runoff election amid tough challenge from Spencer Pratt and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Meta leads largest-ever anti-scam operation with FBI and DOJ, resulting in 63 arrests
events.description: Meta led a sweeping anti-scam operation with the FBI, DOJ and Microsoft, resulting in 63 arrests and the removal of 1.4 million scam accounts.

events.title: Chicago cop killing suspect sent back to jail by judge who previously freed him under controversial bail law
events.description: The accused killer of Chicago police officer John Bartholomew is ordered back into custody in a carjacking case after the SAFE-T Act drew scrutiny.

events.title: Illegal immigrant flashes courtroom grin after allegedly killing baby, mother and grandmother
events.description: Illegal immigrant accused of killing a 2-week-old baby, mother and grandmother in California allegedly smirked during his courtroom appearance Monday.

events.title: Dr. Oz unveils Medicaid overhaul, clamps down on $2B for illegal immigrants and mandates work for able-bodied
events.description: Dr. Oz announces Medicaid overhaul requiring able-bodied recipients to work 20 hours weekly while recouping billions spent on illegal immigrants.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Dr. Oz unveils Medicaid overhaul, clamps down on $2B for illegal immigrants and mandates work for able-bodied

events.title: Former Texas prosecutor praises 'slam dunk' Kohberger conviction, debunks Hollywood myths about confessions
events.description: Veteran prosecutor with a perfect murder trial record says Idaho authorities quietly built a 'slam dunk' case that led to Bryan Kohberger's guilty plea.

events.title: Israeli official says EU sanctions reveal antisemitism hiding behind 'socially acceptable mask'
events.description: Israel's Deputy Foreign Minister accuses the EU of disguising antisemitism as anti-Zionism after sanctions hit Israeli civil society organizations.

events.title: Iranians speak out over possible Trump-regime deal
events.description: Iranians inside Iran express fear and frustration that a potential U.S.-Iran deal could leave the Islamic Republic intact despite economic devastation.

events.title: US ally answers Trump's call on Strait of Hormuz: 'Part of a diplomatic effort'
events.description: Romania allowed access to military bases for air refueling and defensive activities amid U.S.-Iran tensions, its foreign minister tells Fox News Digital.

events.title: Hidden tunnel discovered in Tijuana may have supported cross-border trafficking operations
events.description: Authorities uncover a 265-meter underground tunnel near the U.S.-Mexico border equipped with lighting, ventilation and an electronic transport system.

events.title: Mojtaba Khamenei touts new anti-US alliance as Gulf backchannels seep into Tehran: analyst
events.description: Iran's supreme leader launched a sweeping counteroffensive against President Donald Trump, seeking to rally Middle Eastern nations into an anti-American alliance, an analyst says.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mojtaba Khamenei touts new anti-US alliance as Gulf backchannels seep into Tehran: analyst

events.title: Anti-cartel hardliner channels Trump in bid to end Colombia's leftist era in pivotal election
events.description: Colombia's next president could reshape U.S. counternarcotics cooperation as voters choose between security-first and dialogue-based approaches.
